terry-stand-light-punch.name: Standing Light Punch
notes: Can be rapid canceled
terry-stand-light-punch.notes_sc: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; faster chain timing than 2LP (better vs. DI); more range than 2LP, but less hitstun (cannot combo naturally into 236LP, 214MP, or 236LK)

terry-stand-medium-punch.name: Standing Medium Punch
terry-stand-medium-punch.notes_sc: Moves Terry slightly forward; cancelable into 3 different Target Combo routes; good against opponents in Burnout due to its low pushback

terry-stand-heavy-punch.name: Standing Heavy Punch
terry-stand-heavy-punch.notes_sc: 2 hits; 2f less recovery on block; both hits are cancelable, making it easy to hitconfirm; 1st hit has more hitstun, allowing combos into 236HK; Counter-hit/Punish Counter advantage carries through both hits; short range but good hitbox priority for counterpoking; 1st hit has much less range (only 2nd hit will connect as a ranged poke); when used out of Drive Rush on block, Terry is left slightly out of throw range but can microwalk + throw to beat 4f light normals

terry-stand-light-kick.name: Standing Light Kick
terry-stand-light-kick.notes_sc: Terry's longest range light normal for 5f punishes (but below average for a 5LK); useful in LK Power Charge loops when spaced; DR cancel is delayed until after 1st recovery frame

terry-stand-medium-kick.name: Standing Medium Kick
terry-stand-medium-kick.notes_sc: Terry's main mid-range poke that can also link to 5LK at closer ranges; can easily link to SA1 on Counter-hit; front leg hurtbox is raised on frames 3-20 making it strong against low pokes

terry-stand-heavy-kick.name: Standing Heavy Kick
notes: Produces a crumple stun on Punish Counter, and +16 frame advantage
terry-stand-heavy-kick.notes_sc: Solid poke for chipping the opponent's Drive gauge with its good range and hitbox priority; Punish Counter puts grounded opponents into a free juggle crumple state (+18 before opponent is knocked down); Punish Counter puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles; decent anti-air on frames 14-15 (strict timing to use effectively)

terry-crouch-light-punch.name: Crouching Light Punch
notes: Can be rapid canceled
terry-crouch-light-punch.notes_sc: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; shortest range of Terry's light normals, but naturally combos into 236LP, 214MP, and 236LK; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-6 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

terry-crouch-medium-punch.name: Crouching Medium Punch
terry-crouch-medium-punch.notes_sc: 2f extra recovery on whiff; 1f extra recovery on block; great poke, counterpoke, meaty, and combo tool that can be up to +3 on block; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-9 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

terry-crouch-heavy-punch.name: Crouching Heavy Punch
notes: Forces the opponent into a standing position
3 frames of recovery added on hit or when blocked
terry-crouch-heavy-punch.notes_sc: Forces stand; good anti-air on frames 10-13 but cannot hit cross-up; very short horizontal range; only first 2 active frames are cancelable; if the attack connects on the first 2 active frames, it has 2 fewer active and recovery frames (meaty timing can be up to 2f worse on hit/block if 3rd active frame connects); active frames 4-6 whiff on crouching opponents unless they extend a vertical hurtbox; delaying the cancel into 236PP can grant better frame advantage for combo extensions

terry-crouch-light-kick.name: Crouching Light Kick
notes: Can be rapid canceled
terry-crouch-light-kick.notes_sc: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; good range, hitbox priority, and low pushback for a 2LK make it a great combo/blockstring starter or Drive Rush starter that can link into 2MP/5MP on hit

terry-crouch-medium-kick.name: Crouching Medium Kick
terry-crouch-medium-kick.notes_sc: Very long range low poke with a launching TC extension; DR cancel is delayed until after active frames; moves Terry forward during cancelable frames (makes 2MK~2HK more consistent, keeps Terry closer for Crack Shoot burnout pressure, but makes Power Wave cancels more unsafe)

terry-crouch-heavy-kick.name: Crouching Heavy Kick
notes: Knockdown time extended when attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter

terry-jump-medium-punch.name: Jumping Medium Punch
notes: Has cross-up attack properties
terry-jump-medium-punch.notes_sc: Puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; has a large air-to-air hitbox; only the last active frame can connect on grounded opponents (standing only, strict timing); lower body projectile invuln 6-22f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

terry-jump-heavy-punch.name: Jumping Heavy Punch
notes: Forces a slam knockdown on counter or Punish Counter when hitting a mid-air opponent
terry-jump-heavy-punch.notes_sc: Causes spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents on Counter-hit/Punish Counter; good hitbox priority for close-range jump-ins; lower body projectile invuln 9-23f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

terry-jump-light-kick.name: Jumping Light Kick
terry-jump-light-kick.notes_sc: Fast air-to-air that hits above Terry

terry-jump-medium-kick.name: Jumping Medium Kick
notes: Forces a juggle state when hitting a mid-air opponent
terry-jump-medium-kick.notes_sc: Cross-up with good horizontal range and forward hitbox priority; lower body projectile invuln 7-20f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

terry-jump-heavy-kick.name: Jumping Heavy Kick
terry-jump-heavy-kick.notes_sc: Strongest all-purpose jump-in with good range and horizontal/vertical hitbox priority; lower body projectile invuln 10-19f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

hammer-punch.name: Hammer Punch
notes: An overhead attack that cannot be blocked while crouching.
hammer-punch.notes_sc: Can link into 5LP/2LP with perfect meaty timing; leaves Terry slightly out of throw range when used from Drive Rush

power-drive.name: Power Drive
notes: A combination attack that's quick and doesn't leave you open for long even if it's blocked.
power-drive.notes_sc: () refers to scaled damage when comboed from 5MP; puts opponent into limited juggle state (no follow-up juggles possible); high pushback on block keeps Terry safe if well-spaced; 3f gap on block for a frame trap (but can lose to 3f startup counters/armor moves)

power-shoot.name: Power Shoot
notes: A counter followed by a sequence of attacks.
power-shoot.notes_sc: Puts opponent into limited juggle state; does not combo naturally on grounded opponents (requires at least Counter-hit or meaty timing); can be used in juggle routes; can follow up with Power Dunk on hit only; 5f gap between hits if blocked

power-dunk.name: Power Dunk
notes: A highly damaging transition attack from Power Shoot.
power-dunk.notes_sc: () refers to scaled damage from CH 5MP combo starter; this standard version occurs when buffering Power Dunk early or within the final 2f of the cancel window; KD advantage varies depending on timing (early input gives better advantage); can only be performed on hit; useful for corner carry and oki

passing-sway.name: Passing Sway
notes: A combination attack that can transition into two possible variations.
Fully invincible from frames 20 - 40 on hit only

invuln.full: Fully invincible from frames 20 - 40 on hit only

passing-sway.notes_sc: Invuln applies during recovery on hit only; no collision box during invuln frames; can be followed up with Jumping Lariat or Jumping Knee; follow-ups cause invuln to be extended until the attack active frames (can significantly delay the follow-up for much longer invincibility); 1f gap between hits on block (can be delayed for a 2f gap)

jumping-lariat.name: Jumping Lariat
jumping-lariat.name_ja: ジャンプラリアットパンチ
notes: An attack from Passing Sway that hits the opponent from the front. Hold forward to increase the distance you move forward.
Completely invincible between frames 1 - 25
Considered airborne from frames 14 - 25
jumping-lariat.notes_sc: Follow-up from Passing Sway on hit only (does not combo unless juggled into); can only hit on the same side (whiffs if holding forward for cross-up); -23~29 on whiff if Terry crosses up; full invincibility and no collision box from 5MP~MK is extended until end of active frames; can significantly delay the input to disrupt Perfect Parry timing while the extended invincibility window can beat normals and nearly all reversals (3-9f gap between hits); leaves Terry close enough for follow-up link on hit, and slightly out of throw range on block; will counter-hit a 4f normal but can lose to specials with early anti-air invuln regardless of which side Terry recovers; in the corner, Terry will still connect even if attempting to whiff a cross-up Jumping Lariat; if the opponent walks forward while cornered, Jumping Lariat will whiff regardless of which side Terry chooses (and will also block Jumping Knee); midscreen, Jumping Lariat will whiff if the opponent walks forward with a walkspeed of 0.045 or faster (if the input is delayed, all characters can walk forward to make it whiff)

jumping-knee.name: Jumping Knee
jumping-knee.name_ja: ジャンプニーアタック
notes: An attack from Passing Sway that hits the opponent from behind. Hold forward to increase the distance you move.
Completely invincible between frames 1 - 25
Considered airborne from frames 16 - 25
jumping-knee.notes_sc: Follow-up from Passing Sway on hit (does not combo unless juggled into); can only hit cross-up (must be holding forward); -25~31 on whiff if Terry does not cross-up; full invincibility and no collision box from 5MP~MK is extended until end of active frames; can significantly delay the input to disrupt Perfect Parry timing while the extended invincibility window can beat normals and nearly all reversals (3-9f gap between hits); Terry recovers point blank on hit for a strike/throw mixup, and safely out of throw range on block; will counter-hit a 4f normal but can lose to specials with early anti-air invuln regardless of which side Terry recovers; does not cross up against cornered opponents, but if the opponent holds forward they will automatically block the cross-up (leaving Terry at point blank); this also applies midscreen if the opponent walks forward with a walk speed of 0.045 or faster (though this does not avoid the Jumping Lariat ender midscreen)

fire-kick.name: Fire Kick
notes: A combination attack that can be canceled into a special move.
fire-kick.notes_sc: Puts opponent into limited juggle state; () refers to scaled damage from 2MK combo starter; can always be interrupted by an invincible reversal if DR canceled on block; DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd recovery frame; special cancel is delayed until after 3rd recovery frame

power-wave-light.name: Power Wave (Light)
notes: Smash the ground with your fist and let loose a wave of energy across the ground. Useful for checking an opponent from a distance, and as a stopgap in combos.

power-wave-medium.name: Power Wave (Medium)
notes: Smash the ground with your fist and let loose a wave of energy across the ground. Useful for checking an opponent from a distance, and as a stopgap in combos.

round-wave.name: Round Wave
notes: Smash the ground with your fist and let loose a blast of energy in front of you. Easy to use up close, and allows you to act before an opponent even if blocked.
round-wave.notes_sc: Stationary 1-hit projectile hitbox that can dissipate fireballs or apply meaty pressure; can be perfect parried like a low strike (screen freeze); puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; no head hurtbox on frames 24-48 (while leaning over)

power-wave-od.name: Power Wave (OD)
notes: Smash the ground with your fist and let loose a blast of energy in front of you. Easy to use up close, and allows you to act before an opponent even if blocked.

quick-burn.name: Quick Burn
notes: Reels off an energy cloaked hook before coming down with a downward punch. The second attack is an overhead that can't be blocked while crouching, making it good for breaking through defenses.
quick-burn.notes_sc: 2 hits that transition to a 3rd attack on hit only; functionally 22f startup as an overhead

quick-burn-od.name: Quick Burn (OD)
notes: Reels off an energy cloaked hook before coming down with a downward punch. The second attack is an overhead that can't be blocked while crouching, making it good for breaking through defenses.

burning-knuckle-medium.name: Burning Knuckle (Medium)
notes: Rush forward with a thrusting punch cloaked with energy. Useful as a surprise attack to close the distance between you and your opponent.

burning-knuckle-heavy.name: Burning Knuckle (Heavy)
notes: Rush forward with a thrusting punch cloaked with energy. Useful as a surprise attack to close the distance between you and your opponent.

burning-knuckle-od.name: Burning Knuckle (OD)
notes: Rush forward with a thrusting punch cloaked with energy. Useful as a surprise attack to close the distance between you and your opponent.
Juggle state time is extended when attack lands as a Punish Counter.

power-charge-light.name: Power Charge (Light)
notes: A quick, forward rushing bodycheck. Useful in a combo when canceling from normal attacks. The heavy version can be canceled into another special move on hit.

power-charge-medium.name: Power Charge (Medium)
notes: A quick, forward rushing bodycheck. Useful in a combo when canceling from normal attacks. The heavy version can be canceled into another special move on hit.

power-charge-heavy.name: Power Charge (Heavy)
notes: A quick, forward rushing bodycheck. Useful in a combo when canceling from normal attacks. The heavy version can be canceled into another special move on hit.
Changes to juggle state on mid-air hit
Can cancel into other special moves except Power Charge only on hit

power-charge-od.name: Power Charge (OD)
notes: A quick, forward rushing bodycheck. Useful in a combo when canceling from normal attacks. The heavy version can be canceled into another special move on hit.

crack-shoot-light.name: Crack Shoot (Light)
notes: A quick, falling axe kick. It is very quick, making it easy to use in combos or as a way of checking your opponent.
Considered airborne from frames 7 - 23

crack-shoot-medium.name: Crack Shoot (Medium)
notes: A quick, falling axe kick. It is very quick, making it easy to use in combos or as a way of checking your opponent.
Considered airborne from frames 9 - 25

crack-shoot-heavy.name: Crack Shoot (Heavy)
notes: A quick, falling axe kick. It is very quick, making it easy to use in combos or as a way of checking your opponent.
Considered airborne from frames 12 - 34

crack-shoot-od.name: Crack Shoot (OD)
notes: A quick, falling axe kick. It is very quick, making it easy to use in combos or as a way of checking your opponent.
Considered airborne from frames 8 - 25
Produces a floor bound during a mid-air hit

rising-tackle-light.name: Rising Tackle (Light)
rising-tackle-light.name_ja: ライジングタックル（弱）
notes: A rising, rotating attack. It grants immunity to jumping attacks, making it useful as an anti-air technique.
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 14
Considered airborne from frames 6 - 35

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 14

rising-tackle-medium.name: Rising Tackle (Medium)
rising-tackle-medium.name_ja: ライジングタックル（中）
notes: A rising, rotating attack. It grants immunity to jumping attacks, making it useful as an anti-air technique.
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 10
Considered airborne from frames 8 - 43

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 10

rising-tackle-heavy.name: Rising Tackle (Heavy)
rising-tackle-heavy.name_ja: ライジングタックル（強）
notes: A rising, rotating attack. It grants immunity to jumping attacks, making it useful as an anti-air technique.
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 8
Considered airborne from frames 8 - 53

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 1 - 8

rising-tackle-od.name: Rising Tackle (OD)
notes: A rising, rotating attack. It grants immunity to jumping attacks, making it useful as an anti-air technique.
Completely invincible between frames 1 - 10
Considered airborne from frames 7 - 54

buster-wolf.name: Buster Wolf
notes: Charge forward with a piercing thrust, before firing a burst of energy. Its swift speed and invincibility can quickly turn the tables on an opponent.
Invincible to strikes and throws between frames 1 - 8
Values do not change when the attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter
The minimum guaranteed damage is 30%

invuln.strike_throw: Invincible to strikes and throws between frames 1 - 8

buster-wolf.notes_sc: Incurs a fixed 45f recovery on block (advantage is not affected by meaty timing); transitions to projectile follow-up on hit only; fast and long range, making it great for combos and punishes; puts opponent into limited juggle state and can combo into another SA1 if juggled high enough; if first hit trades, deals 500 damage and puts the opponent into a free juggle crumple state (may be able to follow up with moves like 214HP, Dash/DR normals, or another SA1)

power-geyser.name: Power Geyser
notes: Unleash a volcanic explosion of energy forward. If the attack hits, input a command to transition to Twin Geyser.
Can transition to Twin Geyser between frames 25 - 38 on hit only
Completely invincible on frames 1 - 17
Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 18 - 25
Values do not change when the attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter
The minimum guaranteed damage is 40%

invuln.strike: Invincible against projectiles and strikes with mid-air hitboxes from frames 18 - 25

power-geyser.notes_sc: 1-hit Super-priority projectile hitbox with good anti-air and anti-projectile properties; can follow up with Twin Geyser (3 Drive bars) and Triple Geyser (1 Super bar) on hit only; starter scaling means that follow-ups will be heavily scaled after a reversal or anti-air SA2; can be performed during the last 4 recovery frames of 236MK for a juggle anywhere on screen; can also be input with 21416+MK+HP (cosmetic difference only)

twin-geyser.name: Twin Geyser
notes: Performs another volcanic explosion of energy after the initial Power Geyser. Input a command to transition to Triple Geyser.
Can transition to Triple Geyser or Triple Geyser (Dud) between frames 38 - 42 on hit only
The minimum guaranteed damage is 40%
twin-geyser.notes_sc: Follow-up to Power Geyser for 3 Drive bars (on hit only); can also be followed up with SA1, 214MP, Drive Rush juggles, or more in the corner; the damage on non-Super follow-ups is extremely low when starting from raw SA2 due to starter scaling; attempting to input Triple Geyser follow-up without enough meter results in a whiffed attack that leaves Terry at -3 KD Advantage; no invincibility at all (can be hit by lingering moves like Kimberly's spraycan)

triple-geyser.name: Triple Geyser
notes: Performs a meteoric explosion of energy after Twin Geyser. Useful for when you want to maximize your damage output.
Completely invincible between frames 1 - 139
The minimum guaranteed damage is 40%
triple-geyser.notes_sc: Follow-up to Twin Geyser for 1 extra Super bar; attempting to input without a full Super meter results in a whiffed attack that leaves Terry at -3 KD Advantage

rising-fang.name: Rising Fang
notes: Launches the opponent into the air, then smashes them with a fist cloaked in energy. It has invincibility at the beginning, making it useful as an anti-air attack or as a way to turn the tables on your opponent.
When under 25% vitality
Completely invincible between frames 1 - 16
The minimum guaranteed damage is 50%
Values do not change when the attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter
* Can only be canceled into from special moves
rising-fang.notes_sc: Only the first 3 active frames cause cinematic hit; damage distribution on non-cinematic hit is 350(x6-7),550 with no HKD and less KD advantage; cinematic time regenerates ~2.1 Drive bars for Terry; faster 2f startup when canceled from Rising Tackle, Quick Burn, or Burning Knuckle; final hit whiffs on crouch block (reduced chip and Drive damage)

rising-fang-ca.name: Rising Fang (Critical Art)
notes: Launches the opponent into the air, then smashes them with a fist cloaked in energy. It has invincibility at the beginning, making it useful as an anti-air attack or as a way to turn the tables on your opponent.
Completely invincible between frames 1 - 16
The minimum guaranteed damage is 50%
Values do not change when the attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter
* Can only be canceled into from special moves

grasping-upper.name: Grasping Upper
grasping-upper.name_ja: グラスピングアッパー
notes: Grab and knee the opponent in the gut before throwing them forward. Throws your opponent without switching places with them.
When near opponent
Properties change to the following when the attack lands as a Punish Counter:
- Deals 2040 damage
- Super Art gauge increases by 4000
- Forces a hard knockdown
grasping-upper.notes_sc: Can dash for a +3 corner throw loop that leaves Terry point blank; can also walk forward with manual frame-perfect timing that allows a shimmy (4f timing vs. Zangief, 3f vs. Blanka/E. Honda, 2f vs. Marisa/Sagat); a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

buster-throw.name: Buster Throw
notes: Grab your opponent and toss them behind you, placing their weight on your arm and shoulder. Throws your opponent while switching places with them.
When near opponent
Properties change to the following when the attack lands as a Punish Counter:
- Deals 2040 damage
- Super Art gauge increases by 4000
- Forces a hard knockdown
buster-throw.notes_sc: Side switches; cannot walk or dash for a corner throw loops; can use immediate DR~2MP for corner oki, but this is susceptible to invincible reversals on reaction; midscreen, chasing with Drive Rush gives very weak oki; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously
